title: betula verrucosa f. dalecarlica
intro: Betula verrucosa f. dalecarlica, also known as Dalecarlian birch, is a deciduous tree species that belongs to the Betulaceae family, characterized by its distinctive white, papery bark and slender branches. The medicinal properties of Dalecarlian birch include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which are derived from its bark and leaves. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to aid in skin issues, such as eczema and acne, while also providing relief from digestive issues and menstrual cramps. Dalecarlian birch is often prepared as a herbal tea, salve, or infusio to utilize its medicinal properties, and it can also be used as a natural dye and for its ornamental value.
